scenarios.name: Ceasefire Holds + Blockade Reversed
scenarios.summary: Near-impossible. Requires Trump to reverse blockade AND Iran to stand down. Not the base case.

scenarios.name: Blockade Holds — Crude $100-110
scenarios.summary: DOMINANT. WTI $100.25 confirmed. 230 tankers stranded. Iran threatening Bab al-Mandeb (2nd chokepoint). 2-day gap into Wed F&O expiry. OMCs maximum pain. 23,500 → 23,200 → 22,800.

scenarios.name: CPI Surprise + Rate Cut Killed
scenarios.summary: CPI prints hot today (Apr 13). WTI $100 makes it more likely. Compounds the bear case — banks, NBFCs, real estate all hit. Secondary to geopolitical but additive.

signals.rationale: Works in ALL scenarios. Defensive FMCG, zero crude sensitivity, 3%+ dividend yield floor. Natural anchor in risk-off Wednesday gap. Zero import cost exposure.
signals.risk: Lower beta — lags cyclicals if scenario reverses. Accept as cost of insurance.

signals.rationale: WTI $100.25 confirms thesis. ONGC: every $1 crude rise = ~Rs 400-500 Cr EBITDA. BPCL: GRMs -$9.4, losing money on every barrel, deepening with $100+ crude. Long 2 ONGC : 1 BPCL short.
signals.risk: Surprise blockade reversal (low probability). Crude drop on OPEC+ supply surge.

signals.rationale: WTI $100 + Bab al-Mandeb threat = maximum urgency. 72-hour holiday gap before Wednesday open. 23,500 PE for base case gap. 23,000 PE for Hormuz/Bab al-Mandeb incident tail. Payout 8-20x. Cost 0.5-1% of portfolio.
signals.risk: Total premium loss only if blockade reversed. Decay accelerates after Tuesday night.

signals.rationale: Upgraded 4→5. WTI $100 + Bab al-Mandeb threat confirms energy security thesis. Domestic coal = zero import cost, zero Hormuz exposure. Govt pricing support. ~6% dividend yield. One of very few large-caps where $100 crude is a tailwind, not headwind.
signals.risk: Regulatory overhang on coal pricing. ESG exclusion limits re-rating ceiling.

signals.rationale: WTI $100.25 = ATF cost shock confirmed. Jet fuel 35-40% of operating cost — quarterly PAT flips to loss at $100+. USD/INR pressure adds aircraft lease cost. Wednesday gap down is entry point — wait for gap to establish, then add full position.
signals.risk: Partial fuel hedges may cushion one quarter. Crude reversal on surprise blockade reversal is tail risk.

signals.rationale: USD/INR at 93.73, heading toward 94-95. Every 1% INR depreciation = +0.8-1% IT revenue in INR. Hold existing. Do not add before Infosys Apr 23 guidance — that's the binary.
signals.risk: Global risk-off hits IT discretionary spend. US tariffs (26%) compound macro headwinds.

signals.rationale: Earnings thesis intact (NIM >3.65%, Apr 18). But Wednesday gap-down = banking sector sold regardless of fundamentals. Do not add before Wednesday open. Revisit Apr 16-17 after gap settles.
signals.risk: Gap-down to Rs 680-700 range possible. Hot CPI compounds rate-sensitive pain.

avoid.reason: GRMs -$9.4, now WTI $100+ = losses deepening on every barrel. Bab al-Mandeb threat adds shipping cost. No recovery catalyst until credible new talks OR GRMs recover above $4-5. Structural short, not a dip to buy.
Crude derivatives (TiO2, pigments, solvents) = 55-60% of raw material costs. WTI $100 = margin compression guaranteed. Avoid new longs until crude trajectory clarifies.
Double headwind: hot CPI kills June rate cut + geopolitical risk-off. Most rate-sensitive sector. Wrong timing to add ahead of CPI + Wednesday gap.

avoid.stock: NBFCs — high wholesale funding (Bajaj Finance, Shriram, Muthoot)
avoid.reason: Hot CPI (WTI $100 makes it more likely) removes rate cut catalyst. Rallied on cut anticipation — sharpest correction if RBI signals pause. Reassess post-CPI.
WTI $100 + blockade + Bab al-Mandeb threat = market cannot rally until geopolitical clarity. Asymmetry firmly negative from 24,050. Any pre-Wednesday rally is a sell, not a buy.

calendar.event: Weekly F&O expiry + Reliance Q4 FY26 results
HDFC Bank + ICICI Bank Q4 results (react Mon Apr 20)

tracker.title: US-Iran Ceasefire & Strait of Hormuz
tracker.india_impact: CRITICAL: Trump formal Hormuz blockade (Apr 12). Talks collapsed. WTI $100.25 confirmed. 230 tankers stranded. Iran threatening Bab al-Mandeb (Red Sea) — 2nd major chokepoint. India imports 85% crude — every $10 rise = $12-14bn import bill, +0.4% CAD. OMCs maximum pain at Wednesday open. Nifty: 23,500 → 23,200 → 22,800.
tracker.watch_for: Iran action on Bab al-Mandeb. WTI $105 next level. Wednesday Nifty open. Tanker incidents Mon/Tue. US-Iran back-channel.

tracker.title: US Tariff & India Trade Dynamics
tracker.india_impact: 26% reciprocal tariff on Indian goods effective Apr 9. Nifty -5.9% on Apr 7. FIIs sold Rs 22,000 cr in one week. Pharma, gems/jewellery, textiles, auto components exposed. Combined with Hormuz = double structural headwind on Indian growth.
tracker.watch_for: US-India negotiation progress. Pharma exemptions. FII flow reversal. Rupee past 94.

tracker.title: Q4 FY26 Earnings Season
tracker.india_impact: TCS 29% beat, fell 2% — sell-the-news. Reliance Apr 15, Wipro Apr 16, HCL Tech Apr 21, TechM Apr 22, Infosys Apr 23. HDFC/ICICI Bank Apr 18 (react Apr 20). Earnings must justify recovery rally against crude/tariff headwinds.
tracker.watch_for: HDFC Bank NIM >3.65%. Infosys FY27 guidance. Reliance Jio/Retail growth.

tracker.title: Crude Oil Volatility
tracker.india_impact: WTI $100.25 confirmed. Bloomberg: $110+ if Hormuz fully closes. India imports 85% crude. GRMs -$9.4 — refiner losses. Bab al-Mandeb threat adds LNG import risk. Aviation ATF compounding.
tracker.watch_for: WTI $105. OPEC+ emergency supply. Bab al-Mandeb. India OMC subsidy decision.

tracker.title: RBI Monetary Policy Path
tracker.india_impact: Held at 5.25%. March CPI (Apr 13) is immediate trigger. Consensus 3.4% but WTI $100 pushes print higher. Hot print (>3.6%) kills June cut. Next MPC June 3-5.
tracker.watch_for: CPI Apr 13. Crude trajectory. June cut probability.

tracker.title: FII Flows & Rupee
tracker.india_impact: FIIs pulled Rs 1.04 lakh cr YTD. Rupee Rs 93.73/USD. Blockade + tariff = double headwind. DIIs counter-buying. Past 94 = accelerated exits.
tracker.watch_for: Wednesday FII/DII data. USD/INR past 94. FOMC Apr 28-29.
